mnemonics: I TALK: Iterator: Traverse, Access, Loop, Keep-safe (from ConcurrentModificationException)
keyPoints: An Iterator provides a standard way to traverse elements in a collection one by one.
The `next()` method returns the next element, and `hasNext()` checks if more elements exist.
The `remove()` method is the only safe way to delete an element during iteration.
commonMistakes: Calling `next()` without checking `hasNext()` first, leading to a NoSuchElementException.
Trying to modify the underlying collection directly (e.g., using collection.remove()) during iteration, which causes a ConcurrentModificationException.
Assuming the iterator is repositioned to the start after a full traversal; it must be retrieved again from the collection.
